productBrief.oneLiner: Turn the visible tab into an export-ready color palette.
productBrief.problem: Developers and designers need a direct way to translate the colors visible on a real web page into reusable design and development formats.
audience: Developers extracting reusable colors from live web pages
Designers building palettes from visible interfaces and references
Open-source users who want an inspectable Chrome or Edge extension
productBrief.positioning: A source-available Chrome and Edge extension that captures the visible tab, extracts a balanced palette inside the extension workflow, and prepares it for copying, sharing, or export.
differentiators: Dominance-aware extraction designed to reduce the influence of tiny saturated details
Balanced selection that preserves meaningful color diversity without forcing unrelated hue families
Exports for visual, design, and development workflows: PNG, CSS custom properties, JSON, copied colors, and Coolors URLs
Adjustable palettes with 5–10 colors, HEX/RGB/HSL formats, and auto, dark, or light themes
MIT-licensed source repository with documented automated tests and browser smoke verification

claims.text: VibePalette 2.0.1 is a Chrome and Edge extension.
claims.evidenceExcerpt:
# VibePalette

**VibePalette 2.0.1** is a Chrome/Edge extension for capturing the current tab
and extracting polished, export-ready color palettes.

![VibePalette logo](icons/icon128.png)

## Features

- 📸 **Instant Capture**: Takes a screenshot of your current tab instantly.
- 🎨 **Real-image palette extraction**: Uses dominance-aware RGB bins, cell
 winners, corner sampling, and top-band sampling to avoid tiny saturated noise.
- 🧭 **Balanced palette selection**: Preserves meaningful color diversity without
 forcing irrelevant hue families.
- 🎬 **Cinematic Export**: Save palettes as framed image exports with balanced
 rows, readable labels, and optional color codes.
- 💻 **Dev-Friendly Exports**:
 - Copy palette colors
 - Share as a Coolors URL
 - Export as PNG, CSS custom properties, or JSON
- 🔧 **Customizable**:
 - Adjust color count (5-10)
 - Choose formats (HEX, RGB, HSL)
 - Auto, dark, and light themes
- ⌨️ **Keyboard Shortcuts**:
 - `Alt+R`: Recapture
 - `Alt+C`: Copy all colors
 - `Alt+E`: Export
 - `Alt+S`: Settings

## Installation

1. Clone this repository.
2. Open Chrome or Edge and navigate to `chrome://extensions/` or
 `edge://extensions/`.
3. Enable **Developer mode** (top right toggle).
4. Click **Load unpacked**.
5. Select the `VibePalette` folder.

## Technologies

- **Vanilla JavaScript** (No heavy frameworks)
- **Custom palette heuristics** (candidate extraction and ranking)
- **HTML5 Canvas** (for image processing)
- **Vitest & JSDOM** (for automated testing)
- **ImageMagick** (optional locally and required by CI for real-image regressions)

## Pro…
claims.rationale: The README and package manifest directly identify the product, version, and supported browsers.

claims.text: The extension captures the current visible tab and extracts an export-ready color palette.

claims.text: Repository documentation records 125 passing tests and a successful unpacked Edge smoke test with zero console errors.
claims.evidenceExcerpt:
# Findings - VibePalette

## Research & Discoveries

### Eyedropper Performance

- The popup keeps a shared sampling canvas and refreshes it only after a
 screenshot is captured.
- Pointer coordinates account for preview cropping, and each selection performs
 a single-pixel `getImageData` read from the shared context.

### Storage Safety

- `chrome.storage.sync.get` can return empty objects if keys are missing.
- _Best Practice_: Always provide defaults.
- _Status_: Implemented resilience in `loadSettings` and `loadPaletteHistory`.

### Palette Extraction Quality

- The old hue-first sampling could overfit tiny saturated pixels.
- _Solution_: Use quantized RGB bins, global dominance scoring, cell winners,
 corner averages, and top-band sampling.
- _Status_: Covered by simulation tests and real-image fixtures
 (`coffee.png`, `astronaut.png`, `chelsea.png`, `rocket.jpg`).

### Release Hygiene

- Public release assets need explicit provenance and dependency checks.
- _Status_: v2.0.0 includes fixture license notes, `npm audit` verification,
 and an Edge unpacked-extension smoke test.

### Verification Results

- Eyedropper optimization: Verified that `sharedCtx` is reused. Sampling is now a single `getImageData` call per click.
- Storage resilience: Functions now gracefully handle `undefined` or null storage responses.
- Current palette extraction: `npm test` passes 125 tests, including real-image
 regression tests.
- v2.0.0 browser smoke: Extension popup loads unpacked in Edge, scripts load,
 settings open, action labels render, and console errors are zero.

### 2026-07-10 F…
claims.rationale: The findings and progress logs directly record these verification results.

sections.heading: A palette grounded in the page in front of you.
sections.body: Capture the current tab with VibePalette, extract a balanced set of colors, and move directly into design or development-ready outputs.

sections.heading: Designed to notice dominant color—not isolated noise.
sections.body: VibePalette combines quantized RGB bins, global dominance scoring, cell winners, corner averages, and top-band sampling to avoid letting tiny saturated details dictate the result.

sections.heading: Diversity without forced hue families.
sections.body: Balanced palette selection preserves meaningful color variety while avoiding irrelevant hue families that do not belong to the source page.

sections.heading: Choose the level of detail you need.
sections.body: Set a palette from 5–10 colors, switch among HEX, RGB, and HSL, and work in auto, dark, or light themes.

sections.heading: One capture. Multiple useful outputs.
sections.body: Copy the colors, create a Coolors URL, or export the palette as a framed PNG, CSS custom properties, or JSON.

sections.heading: Source, tests, and release evidence are in the repository.
sections.body: VibePalette is MIT-licensed. Its documentation records 125 passing tests plus an unpacked Edge smoke test covering the popup, settings, action labels, scripts, and console output.

socialCards.headline: From visible tab to export-ready palette.
Dominant color, without the tiny saturated noise.
PNG, CSS, JSON—and one measured color workflow.

carousel.headline: Start with the page you can see.
carousel.body: VibePalette captures the current tab and turns it into a working color palette.

carousel.headline: Let dominant colors lead.
carousel.body: RGB bins, cell winners, corner averages, and top-band samples help keep tiny saturated details from taking over.

carousel.headline: Keep meaningful diversity.
carousel.body: Palette selection preserves useful color variation without forcing hue families that are irrelevant to the source.

carousel.headline: Tune the palette to the job.
carousel.body: Choose 5–10 colors, select HEX, RGB, or HSL, and switch among auto, dark, and light themes.

carousel.headline: Export color where it needs to go.
carousel.body: Copy colors, share a Coolors URL, or export PNG, CSS custom properties, and JSON.

copy.x:
A web page is already a color reference.

VibePalette captures the visible tab, extracts a balanced 5–10 color palette, and prepares it for PNG, CSS, JSON, copied-color, or Coolors workflows.

Chrome and Edge. MIT licensed. Source on GitHub.
copy.linkedIn:
Introducing VibePalette 2.0.1: a Chrome and Edge extension for turning the visible tab into an export-ready color palette.

Its extraction approach combines dominance-aware RGB bins, cell winners, corner sampling, and top-band sampling—designed to keep tiny saturated details from overpowering the colors that define the page.

Choose 5–10 colors, work in HEX, RGB, or HSL, then copy the palette, create a Coolors URL, or export PNG, CSS custom properties, and JSON.

The MIT-licensed source, test suite, and release documentation are available in the repository.

productHunt.name: VibePalette
productHunt.tagline: Export-ready palettes from the visible tab
productHunt.description: VibePalette 2.0.1 is a Chrome and Edge extension that captures the current tab and extracts a balanced color palette. Tune the result from 5–10 colors, choose HEX, RGB, or HSL, then copy it, share it through a Coolors URL, or export PNG, CSS custom properties, and JSON. Its extraction process uses dominance-aware sampling to reduce the influence of tiny saturated details.
productHunt.firstComment: VibePalette is built around a focused idea: the visible page should be enough to begin a useful palette. The extension combines several sampling and ranking techniques, exposes practical color and theme controls, and produces outputs for both visual and code-based workflows. The project is MIT-licensed, and its source, tests, packaging scripts, and verification notes are available in the GitHub repository.

email.subject: Meet VibePalette: palettes from the visible tab
email.body:
A page catches your eye. VibePalette turns it into something you can use.

The Chrome and Edge extension captures the current tab and extracts a balanced palette using dominance-aware sampling designed to reduce tiny saturated noise. Choose 5–10 colors, select HEX, RGB, or HSL, then copy the result or export it as PNG, CSS custom properties, or JSON.

VibePalette 2.0.1 is MIT-licensed and available from its source repository on GitHub.

Explore VibePalette: https://github.com/sickn33/VibePalette

headlineVariants: Turn the visible tab into a working palette.
Color extraction grounded in the page itself.
From web page to PNG, CSS, or JSON.
A measured route from pixels to palette.
Capture the page. Keep the colors that matter.

scenes.title: The page becomes the source
scenes.audienceCaption: The page in front of you is already a color reference.
scenes.visualDirection: Open on an editorial web page framed by warm cream negative space; isolate a few visible cocoa and gold details without yet displaying palette values.

scenes.title: Source-backed introduction
scenes.visualDirection: Cut to a restrained repository composition showing the product name, version, browser scope, and MIT license in a typographic source card.

scenes.title: Measured color evidence
scenes.audienceCaption: Dominant colors lead. Tiny saturated noise does not.
scenes.visualDirection: Animate a measured grid over the captured page; show RGB bins, cell winners, corner samples, and top-band samples converging into a balanced row.

scenes.title: Palette controls
scenes.audienceCaption: Five to ten colors. HEX, RGB, or HSL.
scenes.visualDirection: Reveal the cream-and-cocoa extension workspace with a disciplined color grid, count control, format selector, and auto, dark, and light theme choices.

scenes.title: Export handoff
scenes.audienceCaption: Copy it. Share it. Export PNG, CSS, or JSON.
scenes.visualDirection: Move the measured swatches into distinct output cards: clipboard, Coolors URL, framed image, CSS custom properties, and structured JSON.

scenes.title: Source CTA
scenes.audienceCaption: VibePalette. Explore the source repository on GitHub.
scenes.visualDirection: Return to the complete cream, cocoa, and gold palette beside the product mark and the canonical GitHub repository path; hold for legibility.
